title: Walsura trifoliolata
intro: Walsura trifoliolata, also known as the three-leafed walsura, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Walsura trifoliolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Walsura trifoliolata is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of herbal capsules and supplements.
taxonomy: Walsura trifoliolata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, commonly known as the soapberry family. It is native to Southeast Asia and is a member of the genus Walsura. This plant is also known as the trifoliate walsura or three-leaf walsura, due to its distinctive trifoliate leaves.
